page: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 vector extensions, AVX-512 VNNI, and cryptography accelerations like AES and SHA_NI.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression tests ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores place the instance in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is strong, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%. The server also demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving throughput and small-model LLM text generation, which both rank in the top 25% and top 10% respectively.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D32alds_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage provides high resource density for applications requiring fast local scratch space. This configuration is well-suited for compute-intensive tasks, web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, though workloads requiring massive uncached memory bandwidth may experience a bottleneck.
description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 1890 GB of local storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the instance supports AVX-512 and cryptography extensions. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, though uncached memory bandwidth remains average. This server is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, web serving, and applications requiring fast local storage without the overhead of high-capacity memory configurations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors, dedicated vCPUs, and high-capacity local storage for demanding workloads.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
